topic: Someone discovers their neighbor is secretly wealthy
raw_output:
[story 1]

The incessant hum of the lawnmower was grating on Clara’s nerves. She kept tapping her foot against the kitchen tile, the rhythm sharp and uneven. It had been three weeks since Mr. Henderson moved in, and every morning, the sheer scale of his property seemed to mock her small, tidy bungalow. Today, however, something shifted. As she was retrieving her mail, she noticed a gleaming, obsidian-black sedan idling at the curb, far too luxurious for the quiet cul-de-sac. A thick, impeccably dressed man emerged, and the sheer, effortless air of wealth radiating from him made Clara’s jaw tighten. She felt a sudden, hot surge of frustration. *Just let it go,* she thought, clenching her jaw so hard her temples throbbed. She needed to get through this observation and move on to her actual day.

[story 2]

"Honestly, can't he just keep the noise down?" Mark muttered under his breath, running a hand through his already disheveled hair. He paced the length of his living room twice before stopping abruptly, his knuckles white where he gripped the remote control. Mr. Henderson’s property line was a constant source of irritation; the sheer opulence of the estate next door felt like a deliberate affront to the normalcy Mark craved. He watched from his window as a delivery truck, clearly custom and expensive, maneuvered down Henderson’s drive. Mark felt a restless energy bubbling up inside him, a tight coil of agitation he couldn't shake. He needed a distraction, something immediate to occupy the churning feeling in his chest, because just watching the spectacle was starting to wear on him.

[story 3]

I kept glancing over the fence, a knot tightening in my stomach with every passing minute. It was the sheer extravagance of Henderson’s yard that was getting to me. The imported stone, the manicured gardens that looked like a botanical exhibition, the sheer, unbridled display of resources—it was overwhelming. I stood by the mailbox, my fingers drumming a rapid, frantic pattern on the metal. Why couldn't he just keep his extravagance contained? I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to force the tension out of my shoulders, but the feeling persisted, a persistent, buzzing annoyance that demanded resolution. I needed to see something concrete, anything, to break this mounting sense of unease.
